steps.title: Find the key signal about frame choice
steps.content: Both texts note that Impressionist painters selected painted frames deliberately — either to contrast with the conventional gold Salon frames or to harmonize with a painting’s colors.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A captures this idea by stating that many Impressionist painters were intentional about the frames they selected.
The other three either misstate Degas’s uniqueness, reverse the relationship between painting and frame colors, or claim gold frames were desirable, none of which is supported.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
